label: BREAKING
hook: PUTIN WARNS TRUMP AGAINST MILITARY ACTION ON IRAN
stat1: Russia + Iran signed defense pact in early 2025
stat2: Russia has S-300 systems already in Iran
stat3: First reported Trump-Putin call about Iran
tts_text: Breaking. Russian President Putin warns the U.S. against new military action in Iran, during a phone call with President Trump. The warning signals Moscow's deepening alignment with Tehran amid rising tensions.
caption:
Putin reportedly warned President Trump against new military action in Iran during a phone call.

Worth pausing on:

Russia and Iran signed a strategic partnership pact in early 2025. That document includes mutual defense language, though Russia has not committed troops to Iran's defense in any binding way.

Russia already has S-300 anti-air systems active in Iranian territory. They're operated by Iranian crews but Russian-supplied. A US strike on Iranian air defense could mean striking Russian-built equipment.

Putin's "red lines" since 2022 have a mixed track record. Some have shifted US behavior (long-range missile use into Russia). Others have been crossed without consequence.

The story isn't whether Putin warned. It's whether either side believes the warning will hold.

— The Clam Letter
